SCHMUCKS FIRM WITHIN 20 DAYS. 8) BECAUSE ABRAMS FILED RESPONSES TO THE ABOVE DISCOVERY AFTER THE MOTION WAS FILED, LAMBIE RETAINS THE RIGHT TO MOVE TO COMPEL FURTHER ANSWERS TO ABOVE IF NECESSARY.

Petitiom Fot Determination Of Liablilty Of Trust For Debt Of Deceased Settlor And For Order Directing Successor Trustee To Reimburse Petitioner For Attorneys' Fees Advanced On Behalf Of The Deceased Conservatee.
Off Calendar Dec-06-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Deborah Fox for and with Linda Robbins (Petitioner). Mark Schmuck for Richard Lambie (Trustee). Henry Glasser for Paul Abrams (Objcetor).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions. All Parties agreed to not set bond. The Petition for Determination of Liability of Trust for Debt of Deceased Settlor, etc. was taken under submission with regard to the issue of fees.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement; To Declare Invalid Abrams Family Trust Dated October 31, 2006; To Confirm Validity Of Abrams Revocable Living Trust, As Amended; To Appoint Successor Trustee; And To Confirm Title To Real Property
Off Calendar Dec-01-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa, Court appointed Counsel for the Conservatee. Henry Glasser for Paul Abrams. Robert Temmerman for and with Richard Lambie (Trustee/Conservator).

Mr. Timmerman stated that he was not requesting attorney's fees at this time, that he would not request fees until the house is sold because he could not calculate the contingency amount until then, that he understood that there is a conservatorship and that payment of fees required court approval. In addition, Mr. Timmerman stated that the document for release of the Lis Pendens to change title of the property was prepared and that he would file a petition for fees in the Conservatorship matter when the house is sold. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the written order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse;Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; Conversion;Cancellation Of Instruments Based On Lack Of Capacity;Cancellation
Off Calendar May-05-2010 Continued To May-12-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar May-11-2010 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Schmuck for Richard Lambie. Henry Glasser for Paul Abrams. Lawrence Siracusa for Conservatee.

Mr. Glasser stated that he filed a Supplemental Declaration of Paul Abrams this afternoon and requested that it be entered into evidence. Mr. Schmuck raised evidentiary objections. The issue of the Court accepting/considering the Declaration into evidence was taken under submission. Mr. Schmuck requested a ruling on his Client's evidentiary objections. Mr. Glasser stated that he would be filing an ex parte petition to have Mr. Lambie re-appointed as Successor Conservator (no obejction by Mr. Schmuck or Mr. Siracusa). Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to admissability/inadmissablity of the declarations, Mary's authority to execute the deeds, Mary's power to convey/transfer property, capacity, standing, etc. and expressed concern about having resources available to continue to provide quality care for Mary (Conservatee) in the future. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Written Discovery Responses; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Paul Abrams
Off Calendar Mar-24-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Schmuck for Richard Lambie. Henry Glasser appeared by telephone for Paul Abrams.

Mr. Scmuck stated that in general he was in favor of the tentative ruling except for the ruling on the responses to the interrogatories. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the verifications to the form and special interrogatories and the monetary sanctions. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling with some modifications as follows: 1) Motion to Compel Responses to Request for Admissions-Denied. 2) Request to deem Requests for Admissions Admitted- Denied. 3) Motion to Compel Answers to Form Interrogatories and/or deem objections waived- Denied. 4) Motion to Compel Answers to Special Interrogatories and/or deem objections waived- Denied. 5) Motion to Compel Responses to Requests for Production of Documents and/or deem objections waived- Denied.
6) Verifications to the interrogatories to be produced within 20 days. 7) Request for Monetary Sanctions- Granted. Paul Abrams is to pay $975.00 with a check payable to Mr. Schmuck's firm within 20 days. 8) Because Abrams filed responses to the above discovery after the Motion was filed, lambie retains the right to Move to Compel Further Answers to above if necessary. Mr. Schmuck is to prepare the order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Petition For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse;Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; Conversion;Cancellation Of Instruments Based On Lack Of Capacity;Cancellation
Off Calendar Dec-08-2009 Continued To Feb-23-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Schmuck for Richard lambie. Lawrence Siracusa for the Conservatee. Henry Glasser for Paul Abrams (Respondent).

The Court informed Counsel that the late filings had not been reviewed. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the matter being stayed, the appeal, delay and with jurisdiction. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court suspended Paul Abrams as the Trustee and appointed Richard Lambie as the Temporary Successor Trustee. The Court will take a look at Mr. Schmuck's request for Mr. Abrams immediate transfer of all assets and accounting. The Court was informed that mediation on the Conservatorship was set for February 10, 2010. This matter was continued to February 23, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation and for trial setting if needed.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
